---
title: "Vectors"
# Purpose: Teach beginner vector creation, indexing, summaries, and logical checks.
---

{{< include ../_extensions/r-wasm/live/_knitr.qmd >}}

<!-- Purpose: This lesson introduces vectors with simple plant measurement examples. -->

A vector stores values of the same general type. In plant science, one vector
might hold heights, leaf counts, or treatment names.

## Numeric vectors

```{webr}
#| autorun: true
height_cm <- c(12.1, 14.5, 13.8, 16.2, 15.4)

height_cm
length(height_cm)
mean(height_cm)
```

## Indexing

Use square brackets to select part of a vector.

```{webr}
height_cm <- c(12.1, 14.5, 13.8, 16.2, 15.4)

height_cm[1]
height_cm[2:4]
height_cm[c(1, 5)]
```

## Logical checks

Logical vectors help you filter values.

```{webr}
height_cm <- c(12.1, 14.5, 13.8, 16.2, 15.4)

tall <- height_cm >= 15
tall
height_cm[tall]
```

## Try it

Change the threshold from `15` to another value.

```{webr}
height_cm <- c(12.1, 14.5, 13.8, 16.2, 15.4)

threshold <- 15
height_cm[height_cm >= threshold]
```
